private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Fossa Systems is a Series A space and aerospace company that has raised €6.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same-to-adjacent-stage private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B). Public comparable analysis using six Space & Aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median P/S of 49.8x is not applicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

The peer-median method yielded a $400M implied valuation. This represents the median across the four private comparables, which range from $274M to $1.3B. Given Fossa's Series A stage positioning, the $400M peer median provides a direct anchor without adjustment.

The triangulated valuation range is $340M to $460M, with a midpoint of $400M, supported by the single applicable methodology. Key uncertainties include the limited peer set size (n=4), stage-mixing in the comparables (two Series B versus one Series A peer at the median), and absence of revenue visibility to apply sector-standard public valuation multiples. Any post-Series A fundraising or material business development would warrant recalibration against updated peer performance and market conditions.
